title: Mobile terminal and method for controlling the same
abstract: The present invention relates to a mobile terminal providing one hand mode for distinguishing a view area from a control area on a touch screen and a method of controlling therefor. To achieve these objects and other advantages and in accordance with the purpose of the invention, according to one embodiment, a mobile terminal includes a touch screen, and a controller configured to enter one hand mode that divides the touch screen into a view area and a control area in response to a first control input inputted on the touch screen, the controller, if the mobile terminal enters the one hand mode in a state that a default keypad is outputted on the touch screen, configured to control a simple keypad to be outputted on the control area instead of the default keypad.
claims: Claims ( 18 ) A mobile terminal, comprising: a touch screen; and a controller configured to enter one hand mode that divides the touch screen into a view area and a control area in response to a first control input inputted on the touch screen, the controller, if the mobile terminal enters the one hand mode in a state that a default keypad is outputted on the touch screen, configured to control a simple keypad to be outputted on the control area instead of the default keypad. The mobile terminal of claim 1, wherein the controller is further configured to output a view area pop-up window on the control area in response to a second control input inputted on the control area. The mobile terminal of claim 2, wherein if a control input is not sensed during predetermine time in a state that the view area pop-up window is outputted on the control area, the controller is further configured to eliminate the view area pop-up window. The mobile terminal of claim 1, wherein if the mobile terminal enters the one hand mode in the state that the default keypad is outputted on the touch screen, the controller is further configured to output a conversation information area of a message application on the control area. The mobile terminal of claim 1, wherein if the mobile terminal enters the one hand mode in a state that a home screen is outputted on the touch screen, the controller is further configured to output the home screen on the control area and control a list of recently used applications to be outputted on the view area. The mobile terminal of claim 5, wherein if a third control input inputted on the control area is sensed and the second control input corresponds to a touch and drag input released at a bezel area of the touch screen, the controller is further configured to output an execution screen of a most recently used application among the list of recently used applications. The mobile terminal of claim 1, wherein if the mobile terminal enters the one hand mode in a state that an execution screen of an application is outputted on the touch screen, the controller is further configured to output an editing area among the execution screen of the application on the control area. The mobile terminal of claim 7, wherein the controller is further configured to output a normal area except the editing area among the execution screen of the application on the view area and the control area and wherein a magnified view of the normal area is outputted on the view area. The mobile terminal of claim 8, wherein the controller is further configured to control not only the normal area outputted on the control area but also the normal area outputted on the view area in response to a fourth control input inputted on the normal area which is outputted on the control area. The mobile terminal of claim 1, wherein the first control input corresponds to a press touch and drag input in one direction. The mobile terminal of claim 10, wherein if a second control input is sensed in the one hand mode, the controller is further configured to terminate the one hand mode and switch the mobile terminal into a normal mode and wherein the second control input corresponds to a press touch and drag input in a different direction. The mobile terminal of claim 1, wherein the view area corresponds to an area at which a control input is not sensed and wherein the control area corresponds to an area at which a control input is sensed. The mobile terminal of claim 1, wherein the first control input is sensed on a blank area of the touch screen. The mobile terminal of claim 1, wherein if the mobile terminal enters the one hand mode, the controller is further configured to output a division indicator between the view area and the control area. The mobile terminal of claim 1, further comprising a wireless communication unit, wherein if an event is received, the controller is further configured to output a notification for the received event at the top of the touch screen and output the view area and the control area in a manner of moving the view area and the control area to the bottom as much as a size of the notification. A method of controlling a mobile terminal, comprising the steps of: sensing a first control input inputted on a touch screen; and entering one hand mode that divides the touch screen into a view area and a control area in response to the first control input, wherein if the mobile terminal enters the one hand mode in a state that a default keypad is outputted on the touch screen, the step of entering the one hand mode further comprises the step of outputting a simple keypad on the control area instead of the default keypad. The method of claim 16, wherein if the mobile terminal enters the one hand mode in a state that a home screen is outputted on the touch screen, the step of entering the one hand mode further comprises the step of outputting the home screen on the control area and outputting a recently used list on the view area. The method of claim 16, wherein if the mobile terminal enters the one hand mode in a state that an execution screen of an application is outputted on the touch screen, the step of entering the one hand mode further comprises the step of outputting an editing area among the execution screen of the application on the control area.
